title: Pasqal, A Global Leader in Neutral Atom Quantum Computing, to Go Public via Business Combination with Bleichroeder Acquisition Corp II
why:
- Pasqal, a leader in neutral atom quantum computing, is going public via a business combination with Bleichroeder Acquisition Corp II, valuing the company at $2 billion pre-money.
- Pasqal has deployed 7 quantum computers globally, more than any other pure play neutral atom quantum computing company, with 3 more in production.
- The company is trusted by industry leaders, with partnerships including IBM (as part of the IBM Quantum Network) and NVIDIA, and serves over 25 commercial customers and partners such as Sumitomo, LGE, and CMA CGM.
- Pasqal was co-founded by Nobel Prize Laureate Alain Aspect and benefits from significant scientific leadership and intellectual property.
- The transaction will provide over $600 million in gross proceeds to accelerate Pasqal’s technology roadmap, commercialization, and achievement of quantum advantage and fault-tolerant quantum computing.
